metadata.creator: WIGMORE HALL
metadata.description:
Sir András Schiff：Haydn Festival At Wigmore Hall On 5 March 2022

For the final concert of the Haydn Festival, on Saturday 5 March 2022, Sir András Schiff will play Haydn’s English Grand Piano, Longman & Broderip, London, 1794-5, with a compass of 5½ octaves. Cobbe Collection, Hatchlands Park. Lent by courtesy of the Trustees of The Cobbe Collection Trust. Adopted for 2022 by Sir Sydney and Lady Lipworth. Tuned by Paul McNulty.

This piano was brought by Haydn to his home in Vienna, on his return from London in 1795. It was certainly played by Beethoven and seen, played upon, and described in his journals by Vincent Novello in 1829.

PROGRAMME
Joseph Haydn (1732-1809)
01 Variations in F minor, Hob.XVII:6
02 Piano Trio in C major, Hob.XV:27
03 ‘The Mermaid’s Song’ from English Canzonettas (set I) 
04 ‘Recollection’ from English Canzonettas (set I) 
05 'A Pastoral Song’ from English Canzonettas (set I) 
06 ‘Pleasing Pain’ from English Canzonettas (set I)
07 ‘She never told her love’ from English Canzonettas (set II) 
08 ‘Sailor’s Song’ from English Canzonettas (set II) 
09 Keyboard Sonata in E-flat major, Hob.XVI:52
10 Piano Trio in E-flat minor, Hob.XV:31

CAST
Sir András Schiff fortepiano
Erich Höbarth violin
Christophe Coin cello
Ailish Tynan soprano
